title: silene graminifolia var. viscosa
intro: Silene graminifolia var. viscosa, also known as sticky catchfly, is a plant species belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant has been used to treat various health issues, such as colds, sore throats, and skin irritations, due to its health benefits in soothing and calming the body. Herbal preparations of Silene graminifolia var. viscosa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed as teas, added to bath water, or applied topically to the affected areas.
